title: Dhuandhar Falls
tagline: Dhuandhar Falls — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: The Dhuandhar Falls (धुआंधार) is a waterfall in Jabalpur district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: The Dhuandhar Falls (धुआंधार) is a waterfall in Jabalpur district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chausath Yogini Temple, Bhedaghat
topPlaces.description: The Chausath Yogini Temple, Bhedaghat, also called the Golaki Math ("circular lodge"), is one of India's yogini temples, but exceptionally it has shrines for 81 rather than the usual 64 yoginis. All the same, scholars include it among the 64-yogini temples (चौंसठ chausath meaning 64 in Hindi).

topPlaces.name: Bhedaghat
topPlaces.description: Bhedaghat is a town and a nagar panchayat in Jabalpur district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated by the side of river Narmada and is approximately 20 km from Jabalpur city.

topPlaces.name: Marble Rocks
topPlaces.description: The Marble Rocks is an area along the Narmada River in central India near the city of Jabalpur; in Bhedaghat of Jabalpur District, in the state of Madhya Pradesh. The river has carved the soft marble, creating a beautiful gorge of about 8 km (5.0 mi) in length.

topPlaces.name: Tewar, Madhya Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Tewar is a village in the Jabalpur district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is the site of Tripuri, an ancient city-state, and the capital of the later Kalachuris during the 8th–13th centuries.

topPlaces.name: Pisanhari Ki Marhia
topPlaces.description: Pisanhari ki Marhia is a Jain temple built in the 15th century and located in the city of Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh, India. The temple is named after its creator, a local woman who, according to legend, paid for the construction of the temple with the money she saved from milling flour.

topPlaces.name: Madan Mahal, Jabalpur
topPlaces.description: Madan Mahal is a suburban area of Jabalpur famous for the historical Durgavati fort. The area also has a railway station named Madan Mahal.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Chausath Yogini Temple, Bhedaghat & around

items.note: The Chausath Yogini Temple, Bhedaghat, also called the Golaki Math ("circular lodge"), is one of India's yogini temples, but exceptionally it has shrines for 81 rather than the usual 64 yoginis. All the same, scholars include it among the 64-yogini temples (चौंसठ chausath meaning 64 in Hindi).
Bhedaghat is a town and a nagar panchayat in Jabalpur district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated by the side of river Narmada and is approximately 20 km from Jabalpur city.

The Marble Rocks is an area along the Narmada River in central India near the city of Jabalpur; in Bhedaghat of Jabalpur District, in the state of Madhya Pradesh. The river has carved the soft marble, creating a beautiful gorge of about 8 km (5.0 mi) in length.
Tewar is a village in the Jabalpur district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is the site of Tripuri, an ancient city-state, and the capital of the later Kalachuris during the 8th–13th centuries.

Pisanhari ki Marhia is a Jain temple built in the 15th century and located in the city of Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh, India. The temple is named after its creator, a local woman who, according to legend, paid for the construction of the temple with the money she saved from milling flour.
Madan Mahal is a suburban area of Jabalpur famous for the historical Durgavati fort. The area also has a railway station named Madan Mahal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dhuandhar Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dhuandhar Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dhuandhar Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Jabalpur Junction (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dhuandhar Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dhuandhar Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dhuandhar Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Dhuandhar Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dhuandhar Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chausath Yogini Temple, Bhedaghat, Bhedaghat, Marble Rocks, Tewar, Madhya Pradesh, Pisanhari Ki Marhia.
